mechanism_narrative: Sustained expansion in consumer spending, proxied by retail sales (RSAFS), signals robust domestic demand that drives corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show no significant correlation with RSAFS growth rates (absolute t-stat < 1.96) over any full market cycle.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate remains above 6.0%, it indicates persistent labor market slack that keeps the Federal Reserve accommodative, reducing discount rates and supporting equity prices.
evidence_refs: fact:Federal Reserve forward guidance regarding 6.5% threshold in 2012-2014
failure_condition: During episodes where UNRATE exceeds 6.0%, the average 20-day forward return of SPX is negative or not significantly positive (one-tailed t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Negative real interest rates, computed as FEDFUNDS minus trailing 12-month CPI inflation, signal extremely accommodative monetary policy that compresses discount rates and boosts equity valuations.
failure_condition: When real rates are negative, the average 20-day forward SPX return is not statistically greater than when real rates are positive (independent t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rapid growth in housing starts combined with rising real GDP signals late-cycle overheating that typically leads to tighter monetary policy and negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: After triggers HOUST > 1200 and GDPC1 > 16000 occur, SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production decisively breaks above its pre-crisis plateau (index > 103), it confirms durable manufacturing expansion that lifts corporate profits and drives positive equity returns.
failure_condition: When INDPRO exceeds 103, the median 20-day SPX forward return is less than 0.5% or negative.

mechanism_narrative: Low and stable CPI inflation reduces uncertainty about future policy rates and keeps the discount rate applied to equity cash flows low, benefiting stock prices in the short term.
failure_condition: In periods where CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth is below 2%, 5-day SPX forward returns show no positive average excess over periods of above-2% growth (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in payroll employment and retail sales confirms strong household income and consumption, jointly driving corporate earnings and producing positive long-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The composite signal from PAYEMS and RSAFS has a rank IC below 0.02 with SPX 60-day forward returns over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero interest rate environment, accelerated GDP growth raises expectations of earlier monetary tightening, causing equities to decline as the present value of future cash flows falls.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% and GDP growth exceeds 3%, SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of cases.
